questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke is widely honoured with which title in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke is widely regarded as the Father of Indian Cinema.

questions.q: Which film, directed by Dadasaheb Phalke, is considered India's first full-length feature film?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra (1913), directed by Dadasaheb Phalke, is regarded as India's first full-length feature film.

questions.q: In which year did Dadasaheb Phalke release Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was released in 1913.

questions.q: What was the full name of the filmmaker known as Dadasaheb Phalke?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke's full name was Dhundiraj Govind Phalke.

questions.q: India's highest award for lifetime contribution to cinema is named after which pioneer?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, is named after Dhundiraj Govind Phalke.

questions.q: Raja Harishchandra, directed by Dadasaheb Phalke, was based on a story from which Indian epic tradition?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was based on the legend of the truthful King Harishchandra from Hindu mythology.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke worked primarily as a pioneer of which kind of films?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke was a pioneer of the silent film era in India.

questions.q: In which decade did Dadasaheb Phalke make his landmark debut feature Raja Harishchandra?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra was released in 1913, placing Phalke's debut feature in the 1910s.

questions.q: Approximately how many feature films did Dadasaheb Phalke make over his career?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke made around 95 films over roughly two decades.

questions.q: In which year was the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, named in his honour, instituted?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award was instituted in 1969.

questions.q: In which year was Dadasaheb Phalke born?
questions.explanation: Dhundiraj Govind Phalke was born on 30 April 1870.

questions.q: In which year did Dadasaheb Phalke die?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke died on 16 February 1944.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Dadasaheb Phalke born, at Trimbak near Nashik?
questions.explanation: Dadasaheb Phalke was born at Trimbak (Tryambakeshwar) near Nashik in present-day Maharashtra.

questions.q: Which silent film about the childhood of Lord Krishna was among Dadasaheb Phalke's celebrated mythological works?
questions.explanation: Shri Krishna Janma (1918) was one of Dadasaheb Phalke's well-known mythological silent films about Krishna.

questions.q: Because women rarely acted in 1913, who played the female lead role of Taramati in Dadasaheb Phalke's Raja Harishchandra?
options: A male actor named Anna Salunke
questions.explanation: A male actor, Anna Salunke, played the queen Taramati because women rarely appeared on screen at that time.

questions.q: Watching which film is commonly cited as the inspiration that drew Dadasaheb Phalke to filmmaking?
questions.explanation: Phalke was reportedly inspired to make films after watching The Life of Christ in a Bombay theatre.

questions.q: Which 1919 mythological film by Dadasaheb Phalke depicted the child Krishna subduing the serpent Kaliya?
questions.explanation: Kaliya Mardan (1919) dramatised Krishna defeating the serpent Kaliya, with Phalke's daughter Mandakini as the young Krishna.

questions.q: Dadasaheb Phalke founded which film company to produce his motion pictures?
questions.explanation: Phalke established Phalke Films and later the Hindustan Cinema Films Company to produce his pictures.

questions.q: To learn film technology before making Raja Harishchandra, Dadasaheb Phalke travelled to which country to buy equipment?
questions.explanation: Phalke travelled to England to study filmmaking and purchase cameras and equipment.

questions.q: Before becoming a filmmaker, in which visual craft and printing-related field did Dadasaheb Phalke train and work?
questions.explanation: Phalke trained in photography, lithography and printing, skills that aided his pioneering film work.

questions.q: Roughly how long was the active film career across which Dadasaheb Phalke made his body of work?
questions.explanation: Phalke made his roughly 95 films over a career spanning about two decades.

questions.q: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is presented at which national ceremony in India?
questions.explanation: The Dadasaheb Phalke Award is conferred as part of the National Film Awards in India.

questions.q: Who was the first recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1969?
questions.explanation: Actress and producer Devika Rani was the first recipient of the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1969.

questions.q: Which 1917 Dadasaheb Phalke film depicting the burning of Lanka from the Ramayana was a major commercial success?
questions.explanation: Lanka Dahan (1917) was a hugely successful Phalke film based on an episode from the Ramayana.

questions.q: What was the title of Dadasaheb Phalke's only sound film (talkie), released in 1937?
questions.explanation: Gangavataran (1937) was Dadasaheb Phalke's only talkie, made near the end of his career.

questions.q: Which short demonstration film about a growing pea plant did Dadasaheb Phalke reportedly make to convince backers of his filmmaking skills?
options: The Birth of a Plant / Growth of a Pea Plant
questions.explanation: Phalke made a time-lapse short of a growing pea plant (often titled Growth of a Pea Plant) to demonstrate cinema's potential to financiers.

questions.q: Which of Dadasaheb Phalke's daughters played the young Krishna in his film Kaliya Mardan (1919)?
questions.explanation: Phalke's daughter Mandakini played the young Krishna in Kaliya Mardan (1919).

questions.q: In which city did Dadasaheb Phalke famously screen Raja Harishchandra to the public in 1913?
questions.explanation: Raja Harishchandra had its noted public screening in Bombay (Mumbai) in 1913.

questions.q: What was the name of the film production company Dadasaheb Phalke founded with Bombay business partners in 1918?
questions.explanation: In 1918 Phalke partnered with Bombay financiers to form the Hindustan Cinema Films Company, which produced many of his later films.

questions.q: In commemoration of Dadasaheb Phalke, India issued which type of official tribute featuring him?
options: A national holiday named after him
A currency note bearing his portrait
questions.explanation: India honoured Dadasaheb Phalke with a commemorative postage stamp recognising his role as the founder of Indian cinema.
